segment-text: SMOOTH MALE VOICE (V.O.) Previously, on 21 Jump Street...
Long-haired JENKO watches Slim-Shady SCHMIDT get rejected in the high school hallway.
(to Schmidt) You're a fucking nerd.
Jenko sits with the PRINCIPAL as she holds up a failed test. The other a dumb jock. Jenko sees Schmidt at the police academy.
Not-so-slim Shady? But what kept them apart in high school would soon bring them together.
Jenko slams Schmidt to his back on the mat.
You're good at this, huh?
Yeah, I am.
Jenko fails his test and Schmidt gets an A.
You're really good at this?
Yeah, I am.
Hey, you wanna be friends? And against all odds they forged a friendship.
They train and become friends and graduate the academy.
Teenage the fuck up!
They walk across their new high school parking lot. Wait, they're not supposed to look like believable high school students are they? They look forty. Jenko sits next to DAX in AP Chemistry.
Schmidt and Jenko approach ERIC.
You guys aren't narcs are you? Of course they're fucking narcs.
Schmidt and Jenko take the drug, Jenko dives through the gong, Schmidt holds the racing baton as a penis.
I can't sell all this shit on my own, but I only bring in people I like. And narcs. Like this idiot could get into Berkeley. My daughter got a 1480 on her S, A, T's and got wait-listed.
Schmidt and Jenko have a car chase with the one-percenters. Anyway, there's a car chase, some explosions. They chase Leonardo DiCaprio and Brad Pitt in a water park. I have no idea what this part's
What's happening? Who are we chasing?
I don't know. This feels weird. Then we get a textbook end of second act disagreement.
Schmidt and Jenko fighting on stage.
You think I'm stupid you're stupid!
Worst best friend ever! Which gets resolved in about five seconds.
Schmidt and Jenko get dressed in their tuxedoes.
Will you go to prom with me? Yada, yada, yada, more explosions.
The limo explodes, Jenko takes a bullet for Schmidt.
You took a bullet for me.
You're a goddamn rock star.
And they get a new assignment. We see Dickson proudly telling them.

You two sons of bitches are going to college! Yes! (getting serious) Only one question remains, will their differences bring them to new heights... or tear them apart? That and who gives a shit? I guess that's two questions. I can't believe this is what I do for a living. I went to Julliard. Unfuckingreal. I need a drink...
INT. COLLEGE CLASSROOM - DAY
A professor stands at a lectern in front of the Yin and Yang. In Chinese philosophy, the Yin and Yang demonstrate how seemingly
We slowly PULL BACK from the lectern and into...
INT. FANCY JUMP STREET HEADQUARTERS - DAY

The professor talks on the screen of a laptop, the crest of the University of Phoenix Online posted above his head. Schmidt and Jenko sit at ergonomic fancy desks, watching the lecture on high end laptops.
Listening for coded messages in online lectures is not what I had in mind when Dickson said we were going to college.
At least we're not back in high school.

We should be in a quad right now playing ultimate frisbee with bicurious virgins. Eighteen year old girls literally go to college to learn how to give blowjobs. It's like an elective. (on the computer) At the intersection of the sturdy Oak and the pliant Willow lies opportunity.
Jenko and Schmidt perk up at the coded message. Jenko scans Google maps and finds the intersection of Willow and Oak St.
Got the location. Let's go.
EXT. PARKING LOT - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko walk across the lot.
How come you never went to college? Weren't you the ValeDeLorean or something?
Money. My parents blew my tuition on a time-share in Haiti. What about you? I thought you had a football scholarship?
They took it away because my act scores weren't good enough. Whatever, college is stupid.

Are you nuts? College is where you try new things without being judged and make huge mistakes with no repercussions. It's the four years in between the Abu Ghraib of high school and the Guantanamo Bay of the rest of your life.
INT. UNMARKED SEDAN - DAY
Jenko drives.
Not going to college is pretty much my biggest regret.
Forget college. We're exactly the shit-kicking badasses we wanted to be when we graduated the academy. What else do we need?
I don't know. I just always thought things could be bigger somehow.
Schmidt puts HARDCORE RAP on the radio.
(re: the music) Nuh uh, no way.
You drive, my choice.
No, I drive, my choice. I'm tired of people thinking I'm a Wafrican American.
Jenko flips the radio to club-style UNTZ, UNTZ, UNTZ music.
And I'm tired of people thinking we're auditioning for Magic Mike.
Schmidt reaches for the radio and Jenko slaps his hand away.
Don't slap my hand.
Don't touch my radio.

Schmidt touches the radio again and Jenko slaps him again. A slap-fight ensues and the car swerves all over the road until Jenko accidentally BREAKS OFF the RADIO'S FACEPLATE. The two of them stare at the radio as the opening bars of Total Eclipse Of The Heart comes BLASTING out. Turn around...
Shit. This is your fault.
Was your bear paw knocked it off.
Turn around... They stop at a traffic light and a pair of young girls look into the car and giggle at them for listening to this song.
(mortified) Unbelievable.
Seriously. So embarrassing.
A long beat. They eye each other and shake their heads. Then:
(singing with gusto) Turn around, bright eyes!
(equally fierce) Every now and then I fall apart.
Turn around, bright eyes!
Every now and then I fall apart!
Schmidt kicks in with the heavy air drums...
And I need you now tonight! And I need you more than ever! And if you only hold me tight, we'll be holding on forever!
EXT. UNMARKED SEDAN
The car drives off down the road, Schmidt and Jenko belting Bonnie Tyler with reckless abandon.
INT. UNMARKED SEDAN - A FEW MINUTES LATER
Jenko kills the engine at the corner of Willow and Poplar. Look...
They watch as a massive 18-Wheeler stops outside a warehouse. The driver has a shock of white hair and Schmidt gasps.
That's The Ghost. (off Jenko's look) Marco Gostino. He's the biggest trafficker in the country. What the hell are we supposed to be buying?
Don't know. All the informant said was there'd be a message in the lecture where to make the exchange.
The GHOST and a SCARFACE thug leap out and open the back.
Grab the bag and follow my lead.
Jenko grins and grabs the duffel bag.
EXT. CORNER OF WILLOW AND POPLAR - DAY
They walk over. The Ghost and Scarface clock their approach.
(to The Ghost) Let's make this quick. You got the stuff?
You're Achmed El-Sayed?
Schmidt freezes, then turns to Jenko.
(with conviction) Yalla, assalam alaykum Omar Sharif. Huh?
(to The Ghost) My partner wants to see the goods. (beat) You have the money?
Let's see the goods first.

The Ghost eyes them and POPS a SWITCHBLADE. A beat, then he turns it handle-first and hands it to Schmidt. Schmidt attempts to jump inside but gets stuck halfway. Jenko gives him a boost and leaps in after. They cut open a box.
Schmidt lifts the lid of the box and a giant PARROT flies out and pecks the side of his face. Feathers fly as it ruthlessly pecks at Schmidt's face.
GET IT OFF ME! IT'S EATING MY FACE!
Jenko smacks it off Schmidt and it flies out of the truck.
(gathering himself) Exotic birds. As we expected. (to Jenko) Yalla, yalla, pay the man.

Jenko's hand moves to the gun tucked beneath his shirt when we hear the BLIP from a police siren. They all look up to see a police car pull up behind them. JOSH, a fat, curly-haired cop emerges from the car.
(seeing the cop's face) Oh fuck my asshole.
You guys are blocking the alley. I'm gonna have to-- Oh my stars, is that who I think it is?
Uh, hey, cop, sir, we're all set.
Sir? Don't you recognize me? It's Josh from Hebrew school!
This really isn't a good time.
What are you doing in this truck? I thought you were a cop.
What? Me? You're confused.
No, no, it's why I went to the academy. I figured if Schmidty--
I thought you were El-Sayed?
El-Sayed-Schmidt. I triplebarrelled.

The Ghost eyes Schmidt, Jenko, Josh and then Scarface, and in a flash GUNS are drawn by everyone except Josh. Shots are fired as The Ghost and Scarface race into the cab and start the truck, Schmidt and Jenko still in the back. The truck takes off down the block as Josh heads back to his patrol car and radios in for back up. The 18-Wheeler SCREAMS down narrow streets, crushing cars as it goes. In back, Schmidt is tossed like a rag doll, SPLINTERING boxes and freeing Macaws, Parakeets and Canaries. Jenko stands rock solid, somehow unaffected.

Jenko takes an acrobatic jump off a box to his right, a heroic leap to a higher box to his left, and then miraculously whips himself onto the roof. Schmidt gathers himself and copies Jenko's move exactly. Except he flubs it, missing the second box and snagging himself on some netting attached to the back of the truck. Jenko hoists Schmidt up, netting and all. The truck swerves, flinging Schmidt off the roof, but the netting ensnares Jenko's leg and Schmidt is held aloft. They're tied together.
What are you doing? Not dying.
Get up here!
I'm trying!
Despite being occasionally thrown off balance by the anchorlike Schmidt. Jenko strides forward and leaps onto the hood, and starts PUNCHING a hole in the windshield.

Dozens of cop cars join the chase, exotic birds fill the sky. Jenko grabs The Ghost through the hole in the windshield, and the truck crashes into a tree, sending Schmidt flying. Still tied together, Schmidt rips Jenko off the hood as well. The Ghost and Scarface run off, leaving Schmidt and Jenko behind as two dozen uniformed cops rush forward and aim their guns at them, unaware that they're cops.
INT. POLICE PRECINCT - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko sit in front of Hardy, who simply stares at the dozen POCK MARKS covering half of Schmidt's face.
It's where the bird attacked me. I can turn this way if it helps?

Nobody gave a shit about the Jump Street re-boot when you first came on. Anyone with half a brain thought it was destined to fail spectacularly. But you got lucky, so the Commissioner re-re-booted Jump Street and increased the budget and got you that fancy office across the street. As if spending twice the money guaranteed twice the profit.
Makes sense to me.
Like it made sense to you to wreck half the city playing free bird? (off Schmidt's chuckle) And it made sense to you to dangle from a net while The Ghost escaped?

(defending Schmidt) Hey, he's the only reason we knew it was The Ghost we didn't catch. Did you know he speaks Arabic? Did you know the Commissioner is blaming me for allowing you to be put on a different assignment? She's convinced the reason this debacle happened is you weren't doing the exact same thing you did the first time. She thinks doing the same thing will make you recapture the old chemistry and somehow stop your egos from ruining the whole enterprise. She doesn't get that it's always worse the second time around. After the buzz of the initial success wears off, all the tiny differences you thought were cute and endearing becoming irritating and offputting. You begin to wonder, is my partner holding me back? Can I function on my own? One person pulls away. The other gets possessive. It's a slow, painful unraveling, until one day one of you finds yourself alone
behind a desk while the other is the biggest recording artist in the
We see a framed photo on Hardy's desk of Hardy and Celine Dion performing on stage in gold-sequined pantsuits.

But hey, I don't make the decisions around here, and the Commissioner is adamant that if you guys just do the same thing everyone will be happy. But mark my words, screw this one up and you'll be back riding bikes around the duck pond.
EXT. JUMP STREET HEADQUARTERS - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko stand looking at 21 Jump Street.
The new office is swank and all, but I kind of miss the old church.
They turn and head across the street to 22 Jump Street.
INT. DICKSON'S OFFICE - A FEW MINUTES LATER
Schmidt and Jenko sit with Dickson in his new and improved office. Multiple flat screens surround his ergonomic desk. Dickson tosses them each a folder.
They want the same shit, well here you go. Same identities. Same assignment.
We're going back to high school?
Manimal, you're 40.
You going to MC State.
We're going to college? For real?
Someone's cooking a new drug, it's a chemical combination of Adderall and Ecstasy. Kids take it and get laser focus for four hours of studying, then get high as fuck for four hours.
Jenko looks at the name of the drug in the folder: WhyPhy. Wipey?
Why-Phy. Stands for Work Hard Yes, Play Hard Yes.
Dickson taps the screen and nothing happens. He taps a again.
God damn piece of shit.
It's not a touch screen.
Dickson tries to use the Magic Trackpad, but he moves the entire device, accomplishing nothing.
SCHMIDT (CONT'D)
Schmidt starts a video of a young black girl maniacally flinging paint at a pair of canvases shaped like wings. Whoa, she's wasted.
That's her focused, shitbrain.
The clip transitions to cell-phone footage of the same girl standing in a bikini on the roof of a fraternity.
The crowd roars with approval.
She's awesome. (beat, realizing) She's not gonna end up dead is sh--
The girl leaps from the roof and the camera catches half her fall before shifting to the grass. We hear gasps of horror.
Her name was Cynthia Watson.
Dickson slides across a picture from a security camera of the girl receiving a packet of pills from someone in a car.
That's the dealer. You can't see his face, but look at the tattoo.
Dickson puts two fingers on the screen and pulls them apart.
It's still not a touch screen.
Schmidt leans over and zooms in on the picture. The arm reaching from the car has a hideous tattoo of a guy shooting missiles out of his arm as he drives a convertible.
That's a very specific mythology.
Find the dealer. Find the supplier. I just wanna say, it's refreshing to get a case with a black victim.
Refreshing?
I'm just saying we care just as much as if she was white. It's a tie how much we care.
We need to make sure the drug doesn't leave that campus. It's exactly the same as high school.
Not exactly the same. These girls are of age, so we can-DICKSON Be gentlemen. I'm not gonna be responsible for sicking two old-ass predators on someone's daughter.
Technically, everyone is someone's daughter. Sasha Grey is someone's daughter.
Dickson SLAPS the desk.
Just infiltrate the dealer and find the supplier.
I still think going right to the supplier is a possibility.
Dickson SLAPS the desk again.
Infiltrate the dealer, find the supplier!
INT. KOREAN CHURCH - DAY
Schmidt walks into the church, which is now filled with old Korean men and women praying in Korean.
Hey guys. You mind if I...
A Korean woman smiles and says something to him in Korean as she gestures him over to Korean Jesus.
So, you guys don't mind? No one pays him any attention.

Hey Korean Jesus. Long time no pray. How've you been? Busy probably. North Korea? Nuts, right? So listen, with this whole college thing, I was hoping while I'm there I could take care of this thing I never got a chance to take care of, which is, um, having sex. Like with another person. I know it's weird to ask you, not because you're Korean, I assume Koreans do plenty of sex having, but because you're Jesus, and I'm pretty sure you died a virgin, and that's cool for you. I just, I just really don't want to die a virgin. Anything you can do. Amen. Don't tell anyone. Thanks.
Yo, let's go.
Schmidt turns to see Jenko at the front of the church. He heads over to him, and as he goes, elderly Koreans all start saying Virgin, Virgin, Virgin.
What's going on?
Who knows? Guess they all love Mary or something let's go.
EXT. JENKO'S APARTMENT - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko load supplies into the Schmidts' Suburban.
Infiltrate the deli, find the papaya!
They share a laugh as ANNIE and DAVID Schmidt arrive.
Look at you! All grown up and going
to college. Annie grabs Jenko and gives him a sloppy mom kiss.
Hey, Mrs. Schmidt.
(to Schmidt) Is that what you're wearing? Mom...
In my day we wore sundresses and didn't shave anything. Now you wear cargo shorts and shave everything. MOM...
EXT. COLLEGE CAMPUS - DAY
The Schmidt's Suburban makes its way through a campus exploding with excited college kids and anxious parents.
INT. HALLWAY - DORMITORY - DAY
Jenko, Schmidt and his parents carry boxes and bags, as a pair of beautiful college girls narrowly squeeze by.
And you didn't want to come here. One girl has sweatpants with Juicy written on the butt.
Her ass has an adjective.
They arrive at their suite and walk through a small living area to their room. Jenko stops at the doorway. What's up?
I'm the first one in my family to pretend to go to college.
They share a moment then head into the room together.
EXT. DORMITORY - EVENING
The Schmidts stand by their car, saying goodbye.
Mom, we're fine.
(to Schmidt) I meant you. You want us to wait?
Mom! Just get out of here!
Doug or Brad or whoever you're supposed to be, take care of him. Mom!
Other kids laugh good-naturedly at Schmidt and his parents.
What are you laughing at, that's my child!
Schmidt covers his face with his hands as we transition to... Schmidt and Jenko set up their room, arguing over everything.
I call this bed.
Then I call this dresser.
Jenko refers to the dresser right next to Schmidt's bed. The dresser goes with the bed.
They set up appliances. Jenko puts a microwave on his desk.
It wasn't on the list but I remembered last second.
Schmidt eyes Jenko, then unpacks a microwave twice as big.
It wasn't on your list because it was on my list. That's the point of the lists.
(beat, thinking) We'll put the little one inside the big one and cook things twice as fast!

Schmidt tries to move the mini-fridge, then nearly topples over backwards until Jenko helps him with a roll of his eyes. Schmidt's hangs up posters of women and beer, Jenko's are kitten hang-in-there posters. There's no room to put them all up, so they keep trying to put their's on top of the other's. You stop. Two identical Asian hipsters appear in their door, ALAN and MIKE YANG, one in lenseless glasses, one in a keffiyeh.
Hey guys. We're your suite mates. The Yangs.
We heard you fighting. Is everything okay?
We're not fighting. We're negotiating. It's a process.
It must be hard being so different. We're the same and it makes everything so much easier.
Yeah, we just agree on everything because we're brothers.
We're brothers, too.
The Yangs crack up.
Oh. Is one of you older? They both look older.
We're not. We're the age of college students. Normal freshman age.
You guys are funny.
They're like old-looking versions of Poncho and Burbank.
Oh my god... Totes!
Alan and Mike laugh again.
Are you going to explain the joke? No.
You kind of had to be there.
INT. BATHROOM - LATER
Schmidt and Jenko brush their teeth at a row of sinks.
Do you think we have a compatibility problem?
No way. Whatever that is, no way.
Jenko spits out some toothpaste.
Cool. (beat, thoughtful) I'm gonna go jerk off in the shower. What?
You want me to do it in the room? (eyeing Jenko's bare feet) This is why you need flip-flops. These floors are like ninety percent semen.
INT. DORM ROOM - NIGHT
They sit on their beds looking at course lists on their laptops, their case folders open on their beds.
These course options are incredible. I literally just signed up for a class called Logic 101.
I just want to get this over with without humiliating myself. Why do we even have to go to class?
Because we have to act like real college kids. Don't worry, we'll mix some electives with classes Cynthia was signed up for. She had a double major. Fine Art and--
(reading his folder) Police?! I'll take police.
That's not police it's poli-sci.
Like, Italian police?
Political Science.
Fuck science. I barely had a clue in app chemistry.
Do you even know what the App in App Chemistry stands for?
Apparatus?
Advanced placement. You were taking college level chemistry. I was?
You're gonna be fine.
EXT. CAMPUS - MORNING
Schmidt and Jenko walk through the main Quad, surrounded by students of all shapes and colors.
We'll go to class, ask around about the drug and find the dealer. Easy peasy, just like last time.
INT. LECTURE HALL
Schmidt sits down next to a STONED-LOOKING kid.
You ready to get your logic on?
The stoned-looking kid eyes Schmidt like he just farted.
College man... Work hard, party hard, amiright? I guess.
STONED-LOOKING KID
I wish there was some pill I could take that combined those two things. You feel me? I totally feel you.
Awesome. So... You know where I can get some WhyPhy?
The stoned-looking kid looks around and leans in to Schmidt.
Tsh, yeah, whatever. Dude, you're wearing a badge. (off Schmidt looking down) Wow! Did you actually just check to see if you were wearing your badge?
The stoned-looking kid explodes with laughter.
No. I was just-- I-Schmidt gets up and moves to the other side of class. He sees
an attractive light-skinned black girl and sits next to her.
Hey.
Sucks about that girl who died... Did you know her? Why, because I'm black?
What? No. Besides, you hardly even look black. So now I'm not black enough?
Wha? God, why's this so difficult? Because you sat next to me because I'm black.
No... Barely... Does that make me racist? I don't think I'm racist.
Nobody thinks they're racist.
I bet Hitler thought he was racist. I bet Hitler thought he didn't have a not-racist bone in his body.
She laughs. Maya. ATTRACTIVE BLACK GIRL
Schmidt. Eh, Doug. McQuaid. My friends call me-Nervous. Sometimes.
Well, Nervous, yes, I knew Cynthia.
Oh. Did you know she was into-The LOGIC PROFESSOR WRAPS his KNUCKLES against the podium.
INT. JENKO'S LECTURE HALL
Jenko sits in the back row, listening to PROFESSOR JACOBS.
Welcome to Our American Presidents. Hopefully you have all received the reading list and syllabus.
Jenko frantically tries to write down every word the professor says. His pencil SNAPS as he writes silly bus.
Jenko turns around to see ZOOK, a Jenko-looking motherfucker wearing a Zeta fraternity hat and a football uniform.
ROOSTER, a football player with a red Mohawk laughs.
No, it's Halloween and we wore the same costume.
They both laugh again, and Jenko blushes, embarrassed.
EXT. DINING HALL - AFTERNOON
Schmidt and Jenko reconvene.
I'm in heaven. Astronomy is crazy, I met a hot girl and do you realize this card gives us unlimited food?
Any luck finding the dealer?
Nope. Total strike out. You?
No. But seems like the drug is everywhere.
It is?
I hear you can get it anywhere on campus. Twenty-four seven.
Schmidt looks surprised, then eyes Jenko for a beat.
You think... You think they were talking about why-phy like the drug or wi-fi like the internet? What do you... What? Shit.
(letting him off the hook) That's pretty cool though. Campus wide internet? (beat) Maybe we're going about this the wrong way. The dealer has a very specific tattoo on his arm, right?
EXT. QUAD - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko approach the edge of the ACTIVITIES FAIR. Students pack the quad, which is lined on both sides by stalls advertising student organizations of all kinds.
We'll sign up for everything and then do tattoo recon at each one until we find the dealer.

They see stalls labeled Balloon Puppet Society, Debate, Young Entrepreneurs, Mind-Bogglers, Slam Poetry, MC State Bears... Let's do it. MONTAGE of Schmidt and Jenko doing different activities. QUAD - Schmidt makes balloon animals and high fives peoples' tattoo-less arms as their balloons fizzle out. LECTURE HALL - Jenko stands at a podium opposite a student arguing at a speed almost beyond comprehension.
Jenko high fives the confused genius' tattoo-less arm.
DORM ROOM - Schmidt and Jenko cross two clubs off their list.
PROFESSOR'S HOUSE - Schmidt talks to KEN, handsome, and MERCEDES, a goody-two-shoes who looks like Rebel Wilson.
Up top for ingenuity!
Schmidt gives Ken an awkward high five... no tattoo.
STUDENT CENTER - Jenko plays boggle. Everyone furiously
writes down words. Jenko's list says FOOT, FOOTS and SFOOT. Jenko eyes the other players' tattoo-less arms. DORM ROOM - They cross off more clubs, getting frustrated.
Did you find anything? No.
Shit. College is harder than high school.
CAFE - Schmidt listens to a woman's angry slam poem about her vagina. Schmidt eyes the other students' arms until he's forcibly dragged to the stage. After an awkward beat.

Slam. Poetry. Yelling. Angry. Why am I here? For arms. To disarm you. To undress you. With my eyes. And also my hands. I'm not who I say I am, but I am who I will always be. Your clothes, give them to me?

To Schmidt's surprise, people start applauding. FOOTBALL FIELD - Jenko stand in walk-on jerseys with a few dozen other hopefuls, all shorter and narrower than Jenko. A haggard old COACH stands next to a bored-looking chained up bear - the mascot - and blows a whistle. On the field, the properly uniformed players demolish a crew of walk-ons. The coach blows the whistle again and Jenko takes his place at outside linebacker as the starters chuckle to each other.
God I love walk-on day. Recognizing Rooster as the guy who made fun of him in class,
Jenko's eyes narrow. Zook, the quarterback turns and grins at Rooster.

The ball is snapped and in a flash, Jenko chucks his block and races through the line as Zook hands the ball to Rooster. SLO-MO, as Jenko enters the JOCK ZONE. Everything goes to black but his target, Rooster, and we zip to full speed and Jenko slams his shoulder-pad into Rooster's chest. Jenko rises, staring down at Rooster, who rolls over, grass in his face-mask.
Happy Halloween, motherfucker.
Zook cracks up, loving Jenko's attitude, and Jenko helps Rooster up, looking for the tattoo but a cast covers his arm.
INT. JUMP STREET HEADQUARTERS - MORNING
Schmidt and Jenko stand at the bulletin board we saw in the first movie that had yarn connecting pictures of suspects. Now it's just yarn connecting nothing but question marks.
(after a long beat) You're supposed to be telling me what you know.
I know you should stay away from the fish sticks at the food court. I know it felt pretty goddamn amazing to play football again.
I'ma bout two seconds from pulling my gun. Infiltrate the dealer, find the supplier! It's the same thing!
INT. DORM ROOM - LATER
Schmidt and Jenko air their frustrations in their room.
We need to figure this out. No shit.
You're the smart one.
Why do I always have to be the smart one?
Well you're not going to be the strong one! (beat) (MORE)
How did you we find the dealer so quickly the first time?
His phone number was on a sticker. It was almost impossible not to find the dealer the first time. So let's go find the sticker.
I don't think this guy does stickers.
We could check the yearbook office? That's where-SCHMIDT I tried. MC State doesn't even have a yearbook.
So what are we supposed to do? (beat) We need help. Like experts to consult.
Schmidt looks at Jenko and cocks his head.
Dude, you're a genius!
INT. STATE PRISON - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko are escorted down a row of cells until they eventually reach the cell of a jolly looking MR. WALTERS and a very depressed looking ERIC, both in orange jump suits.
Ho! Turner and Hootch in the flesh! (to Jenko) Man, you are looking fit as ever. Eric, you should get some tips. Look at those pecs.
Um, Mr. Walters, I guess I should apologize about your, uh...
My penis? That old thing. Brother, don't sweat it. I am liberated. You know they gave me a vagina? It's amazing. Wanna see it? No.
SCHMIDT AND JENKO TOGETHER
Eric's seen it. Eric's been all up in that shit, ain't that right?
Guys, you gotta get me out of here.
I'm Eric's bitch.
No, you're not.
(aggressive) Yeah, that's right. I'm your bitch.
Listen, they sent us to college to infiltrate the dealer and supplier of a new synthetic. We need your expert advice.
I see, you want some Fava Beans and a nice Chianti... What's in it for me?
What do you want?
Quesadillas. And maybe some lacy panties. I wanna look pretty for Eric. He's my man!
Mr. Walters gives a few cock thrusts to a cowering Eric.
Guys, I'm gonna die in here.
Shut the fuck up! (Eric cowers) I'm sorry baby. (MORE)
You know I don't mean it, right? (to Schmidt and Jenko) Who woulda thunk it? Me and green penis, in love!
Eric collapses on his bed and Mr. Walters rolls his eyes.
So, we asked around campus but no one would tell us anything.

Cause you look like fucking narcs! College kids aren't dumb like Eric. (beat) Look, the dealer has be out there interacting with the other kids. So hit the big events. A big game or a big party, and blend in. Act like college kids and look for someone where they're not supposed to be. If everyone's outside, look for someone inside, if everybody's upstairs, check downstairs. (beat) And don't forget those quesadillas!
EXT. CAMPUS - DAY
Schmidt and Jenko stand at a round bulletin board kiosk covered with posters and advertisements. Dominating the board are flyers for the upcoming Zeta fraternity rush party.
We gotta get invited to that party.
Wait, I actually know a Zeta.
